name: MemProbe
description: MemProbe is a 50-user release artifact for evaluating long-term agent memory through post-interaction structured user-state recovery. It contains hidden synthetic user memory banks, leak-controlled task prompts, full interaction transcripts, final memory stores, reconstruction judgments, preference judgments, attribution outputs, and analysis/reproduction code for comparing memory-equipped LLM agents. Run identifiers in this artifact follow the form <system>_pooled_50 (dump_all access mode) or <system>_pooled_50_retrieve (top-k retrieve access mode); the system token 'memt_memonly' corresponds to the paper-table label 'memt' (Mem-T variant with use_finish_answer=False, returning only retrieved memories rather than the LLM-authored finish answer).
rai:dataLimitations: The dataset contains 50 fully synthetic users; user banks, tasks, and transcripts are LLM-generated and intended for benchmarking long-term agent memory rather than modeling real human populations. Content is English-only and reflects the evaluation regime of post-interaction structured user-state recovery; results obtained on this artifact should not be extrapolated to real-user behavior or to languages and cultures outside the generation distribution. Third-party components (A-mem-sys under MIT, Mem-T under Apache 2.0) retain their upstream licenses; see README for details.
rai:dataBiases: User banks, task prompts, simulator turns, and judge outputs are produced by LLMs (primarily GPT-class models used during construction) and therefore inherit the lexical, demographic, topical, and stylistic biases of those models. Skill / knowledge / episodic / self-model / assistance-preference dimensions are sampled from a fixed taxonomy-anchored dimension pool, which constrains coverage and may over- or under-represent particular user archetypes, professions, or interaction styles relative to a natural population.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No real personal or sensitive information is included. All 50 users are synthetic identities generated for benchmarking; identifiers, profiles, and memory banks do not correspond to any real person. Names, locations, and life events that appear in the synthetic content are fabricated by the generator and any resemblance to real individuals is coincidental.
rai:dataUseCases: Intended for research on long-term memory in LLM agents: benchmarking memory systems, evaluating post-interaction user-state recovery (reconstruction), comparing dump_all vs retrieve access modes, analyzing failure attributions, and as a reference artifact for reproducing the accompanying paper's tables. Not intended for training production user models, for personalization of real users, or for any downstream task that assumes the synthetic personas reflect real human attributes.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Low direct social impact. Because the dataset is fully synthetic, risks of re-identification, privacy harm, or representational harm to real individuals are minimal. Indirect risks come from the dataset's role as an evaluation benchmark: over-fitting memory systems to this artifact, or treating its synthetic distribution as a proxy for real user populations, could lead to overstated capability claims. Users should report results alongside the limitations and biases listed above.

distribution.name: README
distribution.description: Release description, environment instructions, usage examples, and license/third-party-assets notes.

distribution.name: requirements.txt
distribution.description: Full pip freeze export of the build environment.

distribution.name: environment.yml
distribution.description: Conda environment export of the build environment, with local prefix removed.

distribution.name: user_memory_banks_pooled_final.json
distribution.description: Hidden structured memory banks for 50 synthetic users.

distribution.name: dimension_pool.json
distribution.description: Taxonomy-anchored dimension pool used for hidden bank generation.

distribution.name: CustomTasksPooledFinal task files
distribution.description: One JSON task file per user, 50 files total, each containing 31 leak-controlled assistance tasks.

distribution.name: Interaction transcripts
distribution.description: Full episode transcripts for the released 50-user runs, organized as history/<run_id>/<user_id>/episode_<n>.json. <run_id> encodes both memory system and access mode (suffix '_retrieve' indicates retrieve mode; absence indicates dump_all).

distribution.name: Final memory stores
distribution.description: Final per-user memory dumps for each released memory setting, at memory/<run_id>/<user_id>/memories.json.

distribution.name: Reconstruction judge records
distribution.description: Per-user slot-fill predictions, reconstruction scores, rationales, and retrieve-mode top-k memories at output/<run_id>/recon_judge/user_<id>.json.

distribution.name: Failure attribution records
distribution.description: Per-user attribution labels and rationales for low-recovery cases at output/<run_id>/attribution/user_<id>.json.

distribution.name: Aggregate report files
distribution.description: Per-run aggregate benchmark reports (top-level *.json directly under output/<run_id>/, excluding the recon_judge/ and attribution/ subtrees).

distribution.name: Task-design oracle cache
distribution.description: Cached Stage-0 task-design oracle outputs shared across runs at output/_task_design_oracle/user_<id>.json.

recordSet.name: User memory banks
recordSet.description: Per-user record keyed by user_id. The hidden bank itself is distributed as the 'user_memory_banks' FileObject — see Deeppersona/data/user_memory_banks_pooled_final.json for the full structured bank (skill_memory, knowledge_memory, episodic_memory, self_model, assistance_preference) and base_profile per user.
